description: §55.10 Level 2 — a project-registered message (`registerMessages({.LengthFailed: (field) => ...})`) RENDERS at `<errors of=>`. Typing "A" fails length(>=2); the registered function's output `"name is too short"` renders (Level 2 wins over the Level-3 shipped default; no Level-1 override present). Also exercises the `messages` runtime chunk survival — if the chunk were tree-shaken, `_scrml_message_for` would be absent and the wiring stub would render the raw tag instead.
rationale: §55.10 Level 2 (i18n / brand-voice hook, §41.12): `registerMessages` seeds a project-wide store keyed by ValidationError variant; `messageFor` consults it before the Level-3 default. The registered string is DEVELOPER-AUTHORED and normative, asserted verbatim (`field` binds to the leaf field name "name"). Pre-fix (msgchain-render-wiring-2026-07-03) the `.Variant` enum-tag object KEYS leaked the S66 bare-variant placeholder (`__scrml_bare_variant_LengthFailed__`), so the store was keyed on the placeholder and every lookup missed → the L3 default rendered; the parser now unmasks `.Variant` keys to the bare variant name. The `import { registerMessages } from 'scrml:data'` form is the stdlib-documented usage.
